title: JWT verification silently falls back to a hard-coded signing secret
reasoning: If JWT_SECRET is missing in any deployed environment, Socket.IO authentication accepts tokens signed with the public hard-coded fallback. Anyone with source access can forge a token for an arbitrary user id and join that user's notification room, bypassing the intended authentication boundary for socket events.
reproduction: Start the server without JWT_SECRET set, sign a JWT containing a target user id with `medilead_secret_key_2024`, then connect through Socket.IO with that token and emit `join` for the same id.
recommendation: Remove the fallback secret and fail startup when JWT_SECRET is absent. Use a startup configuration validation step so all auth paths share the same required secret.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this feature, and there is no evidence of a startup/configuration test that asserts JWT_SECRET is mandatory.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that clears JWT_SECRET and verifies the server/socket auth initialization fails closed instead of accepting a default secret.
